page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs supplied by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression is also poor. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average throughput, reaching 654.2 GB/sec for cached reads and 75.3 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

Given its high memory density of 128.0 GB alongside moderate multi-core processing capabilities, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded speed. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and moderate multi-threaded tasks like software compilation or text decompression. The lack of local storage and GPUs, combined with a 3 Gbps baseline network speed, makes it less suitable for high-throughput storage-intensive applications or hardware-accelerated machine learning training, though it can handle smaller LLM inference tasks at average speeds.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 13025.11 and average Redis SET performance. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and moderate multi-threaded processing, while its lack of local storage and lower single-threaded speeds present clear tradeoffs for high-performance computing or storage-heavy applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.4xlarge is a memory-optimized KVM instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
